A pain sharp enough to make you buckle at the [pc.knees] tugs at the bottom of your stomach. Fluid "+(pc.isCrotchExposed()?"trickles down your [pc.thighs]":"warms your [pc.lowerUndergarment]")+". You are giving birth.
You waddle as fast as you can to the medbay. There, telescoping robotic assistants lever you onto the bed and issue soothing instructions, telling you to bear down as the pain comes in waves.

Fortunately, you put yourself in the best possible place to see this through. Hitting the alarm button next to your bed causes a squadron of nurses led by Briget to enter, inundate you with towels and anaesthetic, and give you instructions as the pressure intensifies.

You waddle as fast as you can to the nearest restroom. There’s a med-drone fastened to the wall, installed for this exact eventuality. It instructs you to remain calm as it positions a blanket beneath you, and to bear down when it beeps.

Groaning at the timing, you shed your [pc.gear] and position yourself the best you can in the inhospitable and non-hospital-able terrain. The wish that you’d stayed somewhere indoors and safe hums through your thoughts like a mosquito, but there’s no help for it - you’ll have to deliver the baby on your own.
Spasms wrack you as your vagina dilates and then crowns. What you are pushing out is definitely large, but... smooth. Very smooth. And makes no noise whatsoever.
You groan and writhe, then gasp as it finally and thankfully passes from you, leaving you feeling profoundly empty.
The robots help you up and put it in your arms.
The nursing staff help you up and put it in your arms.
After managing to gather yourself, you push yourself up to stare at it.
It’s an egg. A watermelon-sized ovoid, its shell colored
a bright pink
a subtle grey-blue
a mesmerizing emerald-green
a wholesome light brown
black with jagged lines of yellow
purple with spots of green
a smooth, reflective white
a matte black
purple with dashes of white
a beautiful, sultry orange
a remarkable, psychedelic blend of all sorts of colors
“It’ll hatch in another few weeks, dear,” Briget says after scanning it. “And then you’ll have a healthy baby Shalin "+(1===a?"male":"female")+" on your hands.”
This stupid pregnancy made you waddle around with it for eleven months, only to produce an egg?! That’s absolutely ridiculous!
You will never get over how utterly ridiculous Shalinae pregnancies are. Eleven months pregnancy, followed by an egg. How have they not gone extinct? Well, you suppose you exist, for one thing.

You admire, as you must, this product of many months of gestation, singing and the healthiest binge-eating ever, stroking its smooth, damp sides."+("STEELE"===flags.SHALIN_PREG_EGG_COLOR?" Somehow it’s Steele Tech-themed. That’s impressive all by itself.":"")+" Eventually "+(t?"you hand it off to the nursery staff and their":"the incubation drone arrives, and you hand it off to its")+" masterful care. There’s a big lightbulb "+(t?"they":"the nursery on Tavros")+" can stick it under, no doubt.
You clean yourself up, then quickly disappear into a deep, nourishing rest.

blockHeader("Birthing!")
ERROR: 'Zaika Pregnancy Ends' Status Effect does not exist.
Next

You’ve been feeling ready to pop for days now, so when the clenching contractions hit, you’re less surprised than you are relieved. You try to stagger somewhere soft and clean, but find your [pc.legs] about as effective as limp pasta. You drop to your [pc.butt] and begin to pant like the next breath will solve all your problems. You arrange your [pc.legs] as wide as possible and tear off your [pc.gear], if only to invite some slight relief from the bubbling heat flaring in your chest.

Your codex beeps, the alert transmitting to the rest of the ship. Automated med-bots deploy and whirr towards you with the haste of firefighters, swarming all over to check vitals, offer water to, and even to stroke your [pc.hair]. Lucky thing you thought to stay on your ship for this!

Your codex beeps an alert, but you’re not on your ship and there’s no immediate response. You curse the wanderlust that took you out and about today, and just brace yourself for what’s coming next. You wriggle to the safest, most discreet spot you can spot close at hand and start taking deep, measured breaths as you begin to push.

If there’s one thing you weren’t expecting, it’s the absolute tidal wave of ecstasy surging over you as you push. Your muscles sing with the sort of pleasure that should be illegal, while the zaika-touched elasticity that’s been working its way through your body for the last week turns your dilating cervix from a particularly taut barrier into an erogenous zone too intense to compare to any other spot on your body. Even your [pc.clit] feels numb next to the toe-curling, lip-biting, eye-rolling tempest that sweeps through you as you push your egg, inch by inch, through your vaginal passage.

Your body distends around the mammoth ovum, sweat pouring in torrents from your brow as you gasp for breath. Your back arches and your hips tremble, but slowly, gradually, the fullness of your zaika child enters the world. Her shell is a glistening lavender, polished by the nectar of your euphoric mania. You reach out and stroke the textured surface, feeling the pulsing heat that had been throbbing inside you for so long.
The medical drones let you caress your egg for a few minutes before whisking it off to a temporary incubation space. Others tend to you, wiping you down and seeing to your needs.

Medical drones from your ship finally arrive by your side, called by your codex’s alert, no doubt. Some monitor your vitals and tend to your needs while others whisk your egg back to your ship for incubation.
You take a deep sigh of relief, and try to steady yourself.

It doesn’t work, of course. Your body can’t stop trembling, echoes of your orgasmic birth still pounding in your chest. That was... intense! Do all zaika feel that way when they give birth? No wonder they don’t use their egg-slits for sex: no amount of casual fucking could ever equal laying their eggs!

It takes every bit of an hour for the aftershocks to die down and you can flex your fingers well enough to start strapping your [pc.gear] back onto your body. A beep on your codex tells you that a dispatch will be sent from your nursery to retrieve your egg in short order. There, it will enjoy all the professional incubation luxuries Dad provided for you. Even though the eggnancy only lasted a month, it’ll still be quite a few months until your zaika child hatches. Until then, you’ll just have to keep pushing on with your mission.
You’re no longer in a state of perpetual passion from your pregnancy!
